title: Rich Relaxing Thai Noodles Kanom Jeen
description: A mustardy and glassy-jelly Thai dish featuring lime and miso-marinate, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and is dairy-free friendly in the form of kanom-jeen.
instructions: Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a blender, combine miso paste, lime juice, vegetable oil, mustard, and blend until smooth.
In a large pan, combine the blended mixture, coconut milk, and cooked noodles, and stir over low heat.
Add the chopped Thai basil, salt, and pepper to the pan and stir well.
In a small bowl, mix the agar agar powder with a little water to dissolve, then add to the pan and stir constantly.
Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and forms a glassy-jelly texture.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Serve the kanom jeen chilled, garnished with additional Thai basil if desired.
